text.title: RSpec Testing for Rails Applications Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every RSpec Testing for Rails Applications application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One `describe` per public method, one `context` per branch, one `it` per assertion.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Rails testing field manual. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.
Rule rooted in source: Better Specs convention. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Use `instance_double` not `double` for service / repository fakes.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: RSpec verifying-doubles docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Prefer `build_stubbed` over `create` whenever no DB write is required.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: thoughtbot performance audits. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Avoid `let!` unless setup must run regardless of reference; prefer lazy `let`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: RSpec community style guide. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Mirror source-tree structure in `spec/` for SimpleCov per-file gap reporting.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: SimpleCov docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Use `:transaction` strategy for non-JS specs; `:truncation` only for Capybara JS specs.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: database_cleaner docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
